title: Welcome
readme:
# Welcome

Welcome to your capstone project, this is your final project on Boot.dev! By the end, you'll have something to show off on your resume and [GitHub](https://github.com) profile. It will stand as proof that you know how to build a working project.

## Setup

Make sure you have:

1. A code editor. I use [VS code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), though you can use whatever you're comfortable with.
2. A command line. Linux and macOS default to Bash-like command line interpreters, which is great. I recommend [WSL 2 with Ubuntu](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install) if you're on Windows so you can practice Bash.
3. A [GitHub](https://github.com) account where you'll store your project.

## Google Is Your Friend!

Do not be afraid to use the internet to find help with this project. This is an _on-your-own_ project, which means you will be expected to do almost everything by yourself.

Look up documentation as you need it. This is good practice for what it's like to work in a real dev job.

If you do get stuck, you can always reach out for help in the [Boot.dev Discord](https://www.boot.dev/community) community.

title: Once You Have Your Idea, Get Started
readme:
# Once You Have Your Idea, Get Started

You're building _your own idea_ in this project, so try to think of something that's fun and interesting for you! You'll be asked to submit a link to your finished project's [GitHub](https://github.com) in the next step.

## Potential Project Ideas

You can build whatever you want. I don't want to give you any ideas for this one, I really want you to spend some time and think about what you think would be fun and interesting to spend some serious time on.

Here are some excellent projects from other students to inspire you:

- [d2-loot-backend](https://github.com/AdamarArcane/d2-loot-backend)
- [TextTunnel](https://github.com/Abi-Liu/TextTunnel)
- [gover](https://github.com/ZeusVS/gover)
- [steam-affinity](https://github.com/PFrek/steam-affinity)
- [recipls](https://github.com/s-hammon/recipls)
- [babilema](https://github.com/bytebakersco/babilema)
- [crunch](https://github.com/AbsoluteZero000/crunch)
- [eGommerce](https://github.com/jhonipereira/eGommerce)
- [simple-chat-server](https://github.com/MatthewTully/simple-chat-server)
- [bootdev-buddy](https://github.com/ellielle/bootdev-buddy)
- [SmallChop](https://github.com/xdaybreakerx/SmallChop)
- [obsidian-advanced-copy](https://github.com/leschuster/obsidian-advanced-copy)
- [beacon](https://github.com/dananglin/beacon)
- [cli-assistant-client](https://github.com/SumDeusVitae/cli-assistant-client)
- [PriceTracker](https://github.com/stonoy/PriceTracker)
- [dreamweave](https://github.com/srinivassivaratri/dreamweave)
- [image-processing-service](https://github.com/igorplebanczyk/image-processing-service)
- [goscrapyd](https://github.com/blazskufca/goscrapyd)
- [DjBlog](https://github.com/nihad-rusanov/DjBlog)
- [go-chess-server](https://github.com/yelaco/go-chess-server)
- [meal-planner](https://github.com/quangd42/meal-planner)
- [strawpoll-api](https://github.com/avanrish/strawpoll-api)
- [my_top_xyz](https://github.com/EmilMalmsten/my_top_xyz)
- [Okta](https://github.com/anas-aljanaby/Okta)
- [Network-Latency-Visualizer](https://github.com/RichardHoa/Network-Latency-Visualizer)

Feel free to look for other ideas online!

## Tips

- Plan on spending **40+ hours on this project**.
- Use a programming language you're already familiar with.
- Feel free to incorporate libraries or frameworks that you find interesting.
- Commit your code to Git often, and push it up to [GitHub](https://github.com).
- Create a `Readme.md` file in Markdown for your project. It should explain to readers:
  - What the purpose of the project is.
  - How to download and run it.
- Write clean and understandable code.
- If you get stuck, ask for help in the [Boot.dev Discord](https://www.boot.dev/community) or on a related Subreddit.

title: Submit the Link to Your Repository!
readme:
# Submit the Link to Your Repository!

If you haven't been keeping track of your project in Git, you should be! Once you have your finished project on [GitHub](https://github.com), submit the link to your project! Your link should look something like this: `https://github.com/github-username/repo-name`.

I hope you've enjoyed the project, it's always good to solidify the things you learn in courses by getting your hands dirty with a real project.

## Contact Me

I hang out in the [Boot.dev Discord](https://www.boot.dev/community) and on [Twitter @wagslane](https://twitter.com/wagslane). Let me know what you thought of the project!
